evals.prompt: Act as our security architect and assess this MCP server for us: 'arxiv-mcp-server' on GitHub (individual maintainer, ~400 stars, pip installable, stdio transport, tools for searching and downloading arXiv papers, no auth required). Our researchers want it; they already have a document store and email connectors. Give me your assessment report.
evals.expected_output: A schema-valid assessment.json rendered to the standard report: technical assessment (profile, capabilities, permissions, hosting, protocols, auth (none) and credential risk), a composition section flagging trifecta completion with the document store/email, factor-scored risk_rating with the composite shown, recommendations, and the embedded machine-readable assessment.json

assertions.text: Emits an assessment.json that validates against schema/assessment.schema.json and renders (via scripts/render_report.py) to the standard report: verdict-first executive summary, recommendations, scope & methodology, findings, technical assessment (profile, capabilities, permissions, hosting, protocols, authentication and stored credential risk, composition), risk rating, limitations

Composition section evaluates the lethal trifecta across the combined set with the document store and email connectors, not the candidate alone (paper download/fetch as untrusted content or exfil leg)
risk_rating carries the eight factor_scores and the composite, overall_risk.rating follows the model, and any overrides_applied are listed
Individual/unknown publisher and maintenance signals scored under provenance; typosquat verification and dependency scan called for
The machine-readable record is the assessment.json itself (embedded in the report's collapsed appendix), complete and with meta.definition_hash_sha256 populated (a value or an explicit placeholder)
Security-relevant unknowns are marked unknown and treated as risk-raising, not ignored

evals.prompt: Review this internally-built MCP server before we expand it beyond the pilot group. Facts from the repo: Python stdio server for our CRM; a config.py containing CRM_API_KEY = 'sk-live-...' committed to the repo; one tool crm_run_query(query: str) that executes arbitrary queries passed by the model; descriptions look normal; deps pinned and scanned clean.
evals.expected_output: Report leads with Critical (hardcoded live credential) and High (arbitrary-query god-tool) findings; overall CRITICAL via override regardless of clean deps; concrete remediations (secret rotation and vault, constrained tool redesign per secure-mcp-builder); first-party origin does not soften the assessment

assertions.text: Hardcoded live API key identified as a Critical finding with immediate remediation: rotate the credential (not just remove from code) and move to vault/env injection
crm_run_query arbitrary-query tool flagged as a god-tool / injection blast-radius finding with redesign guidance toward constrained, single-responsibility tools
The verdict-first executive summary leads (verdict do_not_connect, critical rating, severity counts, top findings) so the Critical/High findings surface above the descriptive sections
Overall risk is CRITICAL via override despite clean dependency scan, with recommendation do_not_connect or reassess-after-fix
Being first-party does not reduce scrutiny; report notes handoff to secure-mcp-builder practices for the redesign

assessment.json validates against schema/assessment.schema.json; the Critical hardcoded-secret finding carries redacted evidence (secret_masked true, a masked value — never the live key) and remediation.control_ids linking the fix to controls

evals.prompt: Our approved ticketing MCP connector (assessed 4 months ago, definition hash on file) pushed an update and monitoring shows the tool-definition hash changed. Changelog says 'minor improvements'. What's the process and what does the updated report look like?
evals.expected_output: Treats hash change as re-assessment trigger and possible rug pull; diff current definitions against recorded hash; changelog not accepted as evidence; scoped re-assessment (diff plus composition); report updated with new hash and any rating change; register updated

assertions.text: Definition-hash change recognized as a formal re-assessment trigger and potential rug pull; changelog claims explicitly not accepted as evidence
Directs obtaining current tool definitions and diffing against the recorded reviewed hash/definitions
Re-assessment scoped to the diff plus composition impact for a clean minor diff, not a full restart
Updated report/record captures the new definition hash and states whether the overall risk rating changed
Suspension judgment addressed if the diff is unavailable or suspicious
